programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: A 1966 landslide in Wales killed 116 children and 28 adults, and a 2019 dam collapse killed 115 people in Brazil.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When Air Transat Flight 236 runs out of fuel over the Atlantic, its pilots desperately try to keep their Airbus A330 in the air until they reach land.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: A 1966 landslide in Wales killed 116 children and 28 adults, and a 2019 dam collapse killed 115 people in Brazil.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: The U.S. Aviation Weather Center in Kansas City, Miss.; the importance of accurate weather forecasting to the aviation industry.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: On 10 January 2000, Crossair Flight 498 crashes just two minutes after takeoff from Zürich, Switzerland, while heading for Dresden, Germany, killing all 10 people on board.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A team of experts investigates in detail the carnage of a cargo plane; an anonymous tip helps explain why a cargo plane crashed just moments after taking off from Miami.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Relying on instinct rather than the rule book, three flights end in tragedy when crews improvise on their final approach.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A passenger ferry sinks after crashing into rocks off the coast of a Greek island, killing 82; it leads to a shocking investigation.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Near the end of its flight, Continental Express Flight 2574 suffers wing damage and goes into a dive falling out of the sky.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: When an American cargo plane crashes moments after takeoff, investigators uncover a systemic problem with aircraft maintenance.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A Boeing 767 crashes miles from a South Korean runway; the investigation leads to contradicting stories between the surviving pilot and air traffic control.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Almost immediately after taking off from Madrid International Airport, Spanair Flight 5022 banked to the right, slammed into a creek and burst into flames.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Martinair 495 crashes on a rain-soaked runway in Portugal, showing how weather can doom a landing; American 1572 hits a storm and loses both engines amid a tower shutdown; crosswinds challenge an Air France crew already halfway down the runway.

rating.system: Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía
Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: An examination of three crashes that occurred when pilots battled against their sophisticated aircraft.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A private jet attempting to compensate for a late approach to the airport, slams into an Ohio apartment building just a couple of miles from the runway.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A high-stakes demonstration flight in Indonesia turns catastrophic when a Russian jet slams into a mountain.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A complex recovery operation is launched to find out if a 747 which disappeared into the Indian Ocean was carrying illegal weapons.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Pilots are prepared to cope with a range of emergency scenarios, but failures in training can lead to catastrophe in the cockpit.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A look at three deadly air disasters that are caused by misunderstandings between pilots and air traffic controllers.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: A 1966 landslide in Wales killed 116 children and 28 adults, and a 2019 dam collapse killed 115 people in Brazil.

programme.title: Apocalypse: The Second World War
programme.desc: Hitler unleashes the full wrath of his military might.

programme.title: Apocalypse: World War II
programme.desc: The darkest days of the war are covered; Germany conquers France; British civilians endure the Blitz.

programme.title: Apocalypse: The Second World War
programme.desc: As the war continues into 1941, the conflict goes truly global with the savage battle for Moscow and Japan's surprise attack on Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: Apocalypse: World War II
programme.desc: America joins the war; the conflict rages in South-East Asia, North Africa and Russia; the Nazis instigate The Final Solution.

programme.desc: The death toll rises as the war reaches its conclusion.

programme.title: Apocalypse: World War II
programme.desc: The Allies' heroics keep Hitler from advancing; the Red Army arrives in Berlin.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A commuter turboprop crashes onto hilly terrain in New Zealand; the search for answers inevitably leads to the cockpit.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Joining NTSB and military experts as they work together to uncover how a fighter jet could collide with a civilian flight.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Landing at one of the world's most challenging airports, a passenger plane comes in like a fighter jet and crashes, killing 51 of the 71 people aboard.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: On Jan. 3, 2004, Flash Airlines Flight 604 banks to the right just after takeoff and crashes into the Red Sea, killing all 148 people on board.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Three aviation disasters are all linked to breakdowns in vital security measures.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: History's most terrifying air disasters, and the investigations that followed, are revisited and reexamined.

programme.desc: Take a river tour through some of Europe's greatest cities--Budapest, Vienna, and Amsterdam--on Viking Longship Gefjon.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: Azamara Journey travels the rivers of Southeast Asia, delivering passengers to exotic cities and hidden treasures that larger cruise ships cannot reach.

programme.desc: We follow passengers and crew aboard Viking Star during its two-week tour from Barcelona to its home port in Bergen, Norway.

programme.desc: The new Viking Sea cruise ship whisks guests away on a journey across the Arctic Circle and into the heart of Norway.

programme.desc: Travelling up the coast of New Zealand in style aboard the Coastal Pacific, a train that embodies the nation's spirit of grit and determination; for two years, this ride was shut down due to an earthquake in 2016.
